chunks.text: like we have arithmetic operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly we have another set of operators which are called relational or comparison operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we have done comparisons in math many times

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in comparison we have greater than, equal to and many other comparisons

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so our greater than operator is greater than equal to, less than or less than equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: these are all comparison operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: with that if we have to compare one value with another value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if both are equal or not

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so to check if both are equal we write equal to equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in math we have to write single equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but in coding single equal to is an assignment operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: whenever we have to check if a's value or b's value is equal

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here we use this equal to operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: this is the equal to operator

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: which either returns true value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: true value will be returned when a and b are equal

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and if a and b are not equal then it will return false value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we have equal to equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and with that we also have something a not equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so opposite of equal to is not equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so all these relational or comparison operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: either return true value or false value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: how do they return true or false value?

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: depending upon whether whatever is written is true or not

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: for example let's clear this

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: if we talk about the relational operators

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: let's talk about a and b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like if I print a less than b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so we know 10 less than 5 is not true

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: 10 is not less than 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in that case false value should be printed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so when we print here then we will get false print

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but here if we do a greater than b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so yes 10 is greater than 5

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so in that case we are going to get a true value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now it is not necessary that we only compare variables

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we can also compare values directly

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: like for 5 is greater than 8

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: we know that we will get false value printed

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here we got false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this way our relational operators work

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: now like here if we have 10 both the values

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so if I write a equal to equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in that case I am going to get true

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but if I write not equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in that case I am going to get false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because this statement says that a is not equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: but this is a false statement because a is equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: similarly if we do 5 here

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and now if I run my code

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so this will be true

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: why is it true?

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because this statement says that a is not equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: and this is the right statement because 10 and 5 are not equal

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here we got true

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so the print that is true or false value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the print that is Boolean value

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: is printed on the basis of this statement

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so here the expression that is saying

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: the expression that is saying is it true or false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: here if I write a equal to equal to

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: in that case I am going to get false

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: because a equal to equal to b

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: is a false expression is a false statement

chunks.title: video
chunks.text: so all of these are our relational or comparison operators

text: like we have arithmetic operators similarly we have another set of operators which are called relational or comparison operators we have done comparisons in math many times in comparison we have greater than, equal to and many other comparisons so our greater than operator is greater than equal to, less than or less than equal to these are all comparison operators with that if we have to compare one value with another value if both are equal or not so to check if both are equal we write equal to equal to in math we have to write single equal to but in coding single equal to is an assignment operator whenever we have to check if a's value or b's value is equal so here we use this equal to operator this is the equal to operator which either returns true value true value will be returned when a and b are equal and if a and b are not equal then it will return false value so we have equal to equal to and with that we also have something a not equal to b so opposite of equal to is not equal to so all these relational or comparison operators either return true value or false value how do they return true or false value? depending upon whether whatever is written is true or not for example let's clear this if we talk about the relational operators let's talk about a and b like if I print a less than b so we know 10 less than 5 is not true 10 is not less than 5 so in that case false value should be printed so when we print here then we will get false print but here if we do a greater than b so yes 10 is greater than 5 so in that case we are going to get a true value now it is not necessary that we only compare variables we can also compare values directly like for 5 is greater than 8 we know that we will get false value printed so here we got false so this way our relational operators work now like here if we have 10 both the values so if I write a equal to equal to b in that case I am going to get true but if I write not equal to b in that case I am going to get false because this statement says that a is not equal to b but this is a false statement because a is equal to b similarly if we do 5 here and now if I run my code so this will be true why is it true? because this statement says that a is not equal to b and this is the right statement because 10 and 5 are not equal so here we got true so the print that is true or false value the print that is Boolean value is printed on the basis of this statement so here the expression that is saying the expression that is saying is it true or false here if I write a equal to equal to in that case I am going to get false because a equal to equal to b is a false expression is a false statement so all of these are our relational or comparison operators here we can also check greater than equal to like if I write a is greater than equal to so here we will get true value
